title: Lesson 9 — The Con Artist

words.example: His duplicity became clear when both promises unraveled.

words.definition: sly or cunning intelligence used to deceive
words.example: She won the game through guile rather than force.

words.definition: the use of trickery or clever talk to deceive
words.example: Legal chicanery delayed the trial for years.

words.definition: to conceal one's true motives, feelings, or beliefs
words.example: He dissembled easily, smiling while he plotted.

words.definition: to speak or act evasively to avoid telling the truth
words.example: Asked directly where he had been, the witness prevaricated.

words.definition: not candid or sincere; pretending to know less than one does
words.example: It was disingenuous of her to claim she hadn't known.

words.definition: complete and unwavering honesty and integrity
words.example: A judge known throughout the state for her probity.
Detectives quietly questioned the veracity of his alibi.
A specious argument that fell apart on closer inspection.
For once he gave a forthright answer, with no hedging.

words.definition: clever or cunning devices used to trick or deceive
words.example: The room's calm was pure artifice, staged for the cameras.

words.definition: deceit or a deceptive trick used to achieve a goal
words.example: They entered by subterfuge, posing as safety inspectors.

story: Marlowe had built a career on [[duplicity|duplicity]], winning trust with charm and [[guile|guile]]. His schemes ran on [[chicanery|chicanery]]: he could [[dissemble|dissemble]] flawlessly and [[prevaricate|prevaricate]] under any question, always [[disingenuous|disingenuous]] beneath the warmth. The investigator on his case was his opposite — a woman of unbending [[probity|probity]] whose reports were prized for their [[veracity|veracity]]. She saw through his [[specious|specious]] excuses at once. In the end it was a single [[forthright|forthright]] witness, immune to his [[artifice|artifice]], who exposed the last [[subterfuge|subterfuge]] and brought the whole act crashing down.
